title: Configuration
restricted: You do not have permission to manage configuration.
subtitle: Organizational structure, calendars, and integrations

guide.title: Initial setup guide
guide.completed: The basic setup is already complete. You can reopen this guide whenever you want.
guide.how_to: How do I configure the system for the first time?

steps.areas: Create your company areas and teams in Organizational Structure (below ↓)
steps.collaborators: Add collaborators and assign them an area and manager → Collaborators
steps.kpis: Define the KPIs you will measure → KPIs
steps.periods: Create an active period (yearly, semiannual, quarterly) → Periods
steps.assignments: Assign KPIs to each collaborator with a target → Assignments

guide.note: Calendars and integrations are optional and can be configured later.

checklist.collaborators_desc: Load people and assign them an area and manager.
checklist.periods_desc: Define at least one active period to operate.
checklist.assignments_desc: Assign KPIs with targets to each collaborator.

banners.sheets_text: Import KPI values directly from your spreadsheets, without technical setup.
banners.slack_text: Receive at-risk KPI alerts directly in your Slack channel.
banners.email_inactive: Set SMTP_HOST, SMTP_USER, and SMTP_PASS in the server .env to enable email alerts.

advanced.title: Advanced configuration — Integrations
advanced.hint: Jira, Zendesk, external connectors. Technical administrators only.

integrations.title: Integrations (templates)
integrations.subtitle: Configure reusable templates, targets, and auth profiles.

dialogs.archive_errors: Archive all error runs for this template?
dialogs.delete_errors: Delete all error runs for this template?

calendar.title: Measurement calendars
calendar.subtitle: Define measurement cycles by area or team (monthly, quarterly, or custom).
calendar.name: Name

org.title: Organizational structure
org.subtitle: Hierarchy of areas, teams, and people with calendar inheritance.
org.empty_filtered: No units match the current filters.
org.empty_title: There are no areas or teams yet.
org.empty_hint: Click New unit to create your company’s first area (for example: Sales, Technology, Operations).
org.delete_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete unit "{{name}}" ({{type}})? This action cannot be undone.
org.delete_error: Could not delete the organizational unit

errors.invalid_params_json_editor: The params JSON is not valid. Fix it before using the mapping editor.
errors.invalid_params_json: The params JSON is not valid
errors.multiple_users_assignment: A target with multiple users cannot be assigned to an individual KPI. Use one target per person or remove the assignment.
errors.mapping_and_direct_destination: A target with row-based mapping cannot also point to a direct assignment or Scope KPI.
errors.single_destination_only: A target can only point to an assignment or a Scope KPI.
errors.select_mapping_destination: Select at least one destination to create explicit mappings.
errors.assignment_unavailable: The selected assignment is no longer available for {{key}}.
errors.base_collaborator_unresolved: Could not resolve the base collaborator for {{key}}.
errors.scope_kpi_unavailable: The selected Scope KPI is no longer available for {{key}}.
errors.base_scope_unresolved: Could not resolve the base scope for {{key}}.
errors.target_missing_users: This target does not have a users list to duplicate.
errors.target_min_users: You need at least 2 users to duplicate.
errors.select_template_to_test: Select a template to test the target.

api_errors.template_cron_invalid: The configured cron expression is invalid.
api_errors.mapping_entity_type_invalid: The mapping entity type is invalid.
api_errors.mapping_entity_not_found: The selected mapping entity no longer exists.
api_errors.mapping_items_required: You must send at least one mapping to save.
api_errors.org_delete_has_children: This unit cannot be deleted because it has child units.
api_errors.org_delete_has_collaborators: This unit cannot be deleted because it has assigned collaborators.
api_errors.org_delete_has_assignments: This unit cannot be deleted because it has linked assignments.
api_errors.org_delete_has_targets: This unit cannot be deleted because it has linked targets or integrations.
api_errors.template_not_found: The selected template no longer exists.
api_errors.template_test_required_fields: Required data is missing to test the template.
api_errors.template_test_ratio_required: Ratio templates require the stories query.
api_errors.email_smtp_not_configured: SMTP is not configured on the server.
api_errors.email_smtp_connection_failed: The SMTP connection could not be validated.
api_errors.email_to_required: Enter a destination email for the test.

messages.no_changes_to_apply: There are no changes to apply.
messages.no_users_to_duplicate: There are no users in params to duplicate.
messages.no_new_areas: There are no new areas to create targets for.
messages.preview_rows_unmapped_loaded: Missing keys added to the editor: {{count}}
messages.no_targets_to_deactivate: There are no targets to deactivate.
messages.explicit_mappings_saved: Explicit mappings saved and rows resolved: {{count}}

template_modal.no_auth_profiles: There are no auth profiles for this connector.
template_modal.schedule_hint: Suggested cron for monthly close. Example: 0 2 1 * *.

examples.primary_generic_api: Example: path={path} method={method} resultPath={resultPath} valuePath={valuePath} aggregation={aggregation}
examples.primary_looker: Example: resourceType={query|look|dashboard|dashboard_element|inline_query} resourceId={resourceId} dashboardElementId={dashboardElementId} resultFormat={resultFormat} resultPath={resultPath} valuePath={valuePath}
examples.secondary_generic_api: Optional. Only if you want to document another placeholder block.
examples.secondary_looker: Optional. For inline_query use queryBody. For dashboards you can use dashboardElementId, dashboardElementTitle, or dashboardElementIndex.
examples.secondary_default: Example: use the same config A or split A/B if you need two aggregations.
examples.single_generic_api: Example: path={path} method={method} query={query} resultPath={resultPath} valuePath={valuePath} aggregation={aggregation}
examples.single_looker: Example: resourceType={query|look|dashboard|dashboard_element|inline_query} resourceId={resourceId} dashboardElementId={dashboardElementId} dashboardElementTitle={dashboardElementTitle} resultFormat={resultFormat} resultPath={resultPath} valuePath={valuePath}
examples.single_default: Example: sheetKey={sheetKey} range={range} areaColumn={areaColumn} collaboratorColumn={collaboratorColumn} kpiColumn={kpiColumn}. In params: collaboratorValue + valueColumn or valueColumnFromPeriod=true + valueColumnPeriodFormat=YYYYMM

template_modal.placeholders_hint: Suggestion: use placeholders (e.g. {baseFilter}, {from}, {to}) so the template remains reusable.
template_modal.time_placeholders_hint: Suggestion: include {from} and {to} to filter by period.
template_modal.literal_values_hint: Suggestion: avoid fixed values (Story/Bug/Done). Put them in the Target with placeholders.

target_modal.assignment_destination_hint: Select a base assignment only if the target must write to a collaborator KPI.
target_modal.scope_kpi_destination_hint: Select this if the target must write to an organizational KPI. Only one of the two destinations can be informed.
target_modal.raw_jql_hint: Use it only as help to convert a JQL into JSON params.
target_modal.params_json_hint: This content is what gets persisted and used by the runner when executing the target.
target_modal.sheets_params_hint: For leader-based sheets: use range with A1 notation (e.g. 'Alexis Cantenys'!A3:Z), collaboratorValue to choose the correct row and, if months are columns, valueColumnFromPeriod=true + valueColumnPeriodFormat=YYYYMM.
target_modal.explicit_mapping_hint: To update multiple destinations from a single run, you can use targetMap, mappingKeyPath, mappingValuePath, and mappingOwnerType (assignment or scopeKpi).
target_modal.explicit_mapping_hint_looker: To update multiple destinations from a single run, you can use targetMap, mappingKeyPath, mappingValuePath, and mappingOwnerType (assignment or scopeKpi). Looker also supports resourceType dashboard and dashboard_element.
target_modal.mapping_editor_hint: Build the targetMap from the UI. If you use this editor, remove the direct destination from the target. When importing from preview, the system tries to autocomplete matches by name.
target_modal.no_mapping_rows: No configured rows yet. Add a row to map external keys to assignments or Scope KPIs.
target_modal.unresolved_warning: There are {{count}} targetMap rows without an assigned destination. Complete them or remove the ones you do not want to use.
target_modal.explicit_mapping_intro: You can create explicit data_source_mappings for {{source}} without leaving the modal. This stores the external key for future previews and also resolves these rows now.
target_modal.target_subperiod_later: Target sub-period: it will be resolved on execution (no associated calendar or sub-periods).
target_modal.assignment_subperiod_warning: This assignment belongs to a specific sub-period. To resolve the month automatically, select the base assignment (without sub-period).
target_modal.scope_kpi_subperiod_warning: This Scope KPI belongs to a specific sub-period. If you want automatic monthly resolution, use a base Scope KPI without sub-period.

explicit_row.assignment_hint: The key will be stored against the base collaborator of the selected assignment.
explicit_row.scope_kpi_hint: The key will be stored against the base org scope of the selected Scope KPI.

target_modal.preview_hint: The preview uses the selected template and the current modal params, without needing to save the target first.

errors.assignment_blocked: There are multiple users in params. For an individual assignment, create one target per person or remove the assignment.
errors.invalid_json: The params JSON is not valid.
errors.missing_required_params: Required params are missing for the template:
errors.both_destinations: The target cannot point to an assignment and a Scope KPI at the same time.
errors.direct_destination_with_mapping: If you configure row-based mapping, remove the direct destination from the target. Each row must resolve its own assignment or Scope KPI.

target_wizard.hint: Select the collaborator and, if applicable, the destination assignment. One target will be created for each user.

auth_profile_hints.jira_xray: Jira/Xray: use email + API token (Basic) or Bearer token.
auth_profile_hints.sheets: Google Sheets: supports leader-based sheets with KPI/Area/Collaborator and months in columns. If the sheet is public, you can leave it without auth or use an API Key.
auth_profile_hints.generic_api: Generic API: use a base endpoint + Bearer, API Key, Basic, or no auth, depending on the source.
auth_profile_hints.looker: Looker: use the instance base endpoint and auth by token or clientId/clientSecret.
auth_profile_hints.manual: Manual/CSV does not require an auth profile.

auth_modal.looker_hint: If you load clientId/clientSecret, KPI Manager performs POST /api/4.0/login. You can also use a direct token.

subperiod_modal.no_periods_hint: There are no created periods yet. First create one in the Periods screen.
subperiod_modal.select_period_hint: Select a period to view sub-periods.
subperiod_modal.empty: There are no sub-periods for this calendar and period.

scope_kpi_form.weight_hint: Weight of this KPI inside the area (1–100%). The sum of all area KPIs should be 100%.
scope_kpi_form.mixed_note: The manual or integrated value will be used as the direct component. Aggregation from links will complete the aggregated component.
scope_kpi_form.error_required: Complete name, KPI, area/team, and period
scope_kpi_form.error_mixed_weight: In mixed mode, direct and aggregated weights must add up to more than 0
scope_kpi_form.error_save: Could not save the Group KPI

scope_kpi_detail.mixed_note: The final value combines the direct and aggregated components. Manual or imported measurements feed the direct component; recalculation from links updates the aggregated component.
scope_kpi_detail.no_links: This Scope KPI does not have configured links yet.
scope_kpi_detail.no_runs: There are no aggregation runs for this Scope KPI.
scope_kpi_detail.no_measurements: There are no recent measurements for this Scope KPI.

sheets_wizard.title: Connect Google Sheets
sheets_wizard.subtitle: Import KPI values directly from your spreadsheet

errors.column_required: Select the column with the KPI value
errors.bulk_name_required: Fill in the spreadsheet name for every collaborator

steps.url_hint: Open your Google Sheet and copy the full URL from the browser bar.
steps.tab_hint: Select the sheet tab that contains the KPI values.
steps.column_title: Which column contains the KPI value?
steps.column_hint: Choose the column that contains the number you want to use as the measurement.
steps.kpi_title: Which KPI should receive the data?
steps.schedule_hint: The app will read the spreadsheet automatically at that frequency and update the KPI.

fields.api_key_optional: (optional if it is configured on the server)
fields.aggregation: If there are multiple rows, use:
fields.agent_column: Is there a column that identifies each person?
fields.agent_column_optional: (optional - to connect several agents at once)

placeholders.search_scope: Search by area or KPI name...
placeholders.single_value_option: - No, the spreadsheet has a single value -

hints.public_or_api_key: The spreadsheet must be public or your API key must have access to it.
hints.api_key_help: How to get an API Key ↗
hints.no_tabs: No tabs were found. Check the URL and the API key.
hints.agent_column_hint: The system will filter each row by agent name. In the next step you will choose which collaborators to connect.
hints.scope_destination: The data goes to the consolidated area or team KPI. Use this when a single spreadsheet has the aggregated value for the whole group.
hints.bulk_destination: Select the collaborators to connect. The system will filter each row using the {{column}} column. You can edit the name if it does not match the spreadsheet exactly.
hints.single_destination: The data goes to one specific person. Use this when the spreadsheet has a single value.
hints.empty_search: No KPIs were found with that name.
hints.next_sync: The next automatic synchronization will occur according to the configured schedule.
hints.manual_run: You can also run it manually from the Integrations section.

sheets_wizard.bulk_selection_one: {{count}} collaborator selected - {{count}} integration will be created.
sheets_wizard.bulk_selection_other: {{count}} collaborators selected - {{count}} integrations will be created.

done.bulk_message: Your {{tab}} spreadsheet is now connected to {{count}} collaborators filtered by the {{column}} column.
done.single_message: Your {{tab}} spreadsheet is now connected to KPI {{kpi}}.

slack_wizard.title: Connect Slack
slack_wizard.subtitle: Receive KPI alerts directly in your channel

errors.test: Could not send the test message
errors.url_invalid: The URL must start with https://hooks.slack.com/

status.configured_hint: Alerts are sent to the configured webhook:
status.change_channel_hint: To change the channel, disconnect Slack and configure it again.
status.not_configured_hint: Connect your Slack workspace to receive automatic alerts when:

benefits.missing: A collaborator has not entered their values
benefits.deadline: A period is about to expire

instructions.title: Create an Incoming Webhook in Slack
instructions.hint: Follow these steps in Slack. It takes less than 2 minutes:
instructions.step_1: Open <link>api.slack.com/apps</link> and sign in with your Slack account.
instructions.step_2: Click Create New App and choose From scratch.
instructions.step_3: Give it a name, for example KPI Manager, and choose your workspace.
instructions.step_4: In the left menu, click Incoming Webhooks.
instructions.step_6: Click Add New Webhook to Workspace, choose the channel where you want to receive alerts, and confirm.
instructions.step_7: Copy the generated URL. It starts with https://hooks.slack.com/services/....

paste.title: Paste the webhook URL
paste.hint: Copy the URL from your Slack app page and paste it here.
paste.label: Webhook URL
paste.security_hint: The URL is stored securely. It is not shown in full once saved.

test.title: Test the connection
test.hint: Webhook saved. Send a test message to verify it reaches your Slack channel.
test.preview_hint: You will receive alerts here when KPIs are at risk...

done.title: Slack connected!
done.message: The test message was sent successfully. From now on you will receive automatic alerts in your Slack channel.
done.hint: Alerts are triggered when data changes: at-risk KPIs, missing values, or periods close to expiration.

done.title: System configured!
done.message: You can now start loading data and reviewing results.

org.title: Define the organizational structure
org.description: Create your company areas and teams. This defines who is responsible for each KPI.

kpis.title: Create your company KPIs
kpis.description: Define what you will measure: sales, resolved tickets, NPS, delivery compliance, and more.

period.title: Create an active period
period.description: Define the evaluation cycle (yearly, semiannual, quarterly) and its sub-periods.

assignments.title: Assign KPIs to collaborators
assignments.description: Define which KPI each person owns, including its target and evaluation weight.

scope_modal.inactive_hint: Inactive units do not appear in new assignments.

type_hints.team: Sub-group within an area: Technical Support, B2B Sales…
type_hints.person: Individual person as a structure unit.

scope_modal.parent_hint: Choose the unit this one depends on. Leave empty if it is a root area.
scope_modal.calendar_hint: Defines KPI measurement frequency (monthly, quarterly, etc.). If unchanged, it inherits from the parent.
scope_modal.advanced_hint: Only if you use external connectors (Jira, Sheets, etc.)
scope_modal.integration_params_hint: Technical inheritable parameters for integrations. Leave empty if you do not use connectors.
scope_modal.connector_alias_hint: Name under which this unit appears in {{source}}. You can enter several separated by commas.
